observations.recommendation:
1.8	We recommend the Municipality has overstated its asset account by erroneously debiting the CIP – Buildings & Other Structures by ₱3,854,393.55 and understating the expense account Consultancy Services by the same amount.
If the books are already closed:
Government Equity		       3,854,393.55
CIP – Buildings and Other Structures	   3,854,393.55
If the books are not yet closed:
Consultancy Services	       3,854,393.55
CIP – Buildings and Other Structures		3,854,393.55

2.6	We recommended the Management direct the Municipal Accountant to determine the unidentified book reconciling items before an adjustment is drawn.  Furthermore, prepare an adjusting journal entry for those identified reconciling items by debiting appropriate expense account and credited to cash in bank account to present the true value of Cash balance at year-end.

3.7	We recommended that Municipal Accounting Office of adherence of the Municipality to the IPSAS provisions about the recording of the construction materials procured to their appropriate inventory accounts cast doubt as to the accuracy of its financial statements to the extent of the unutilized items which should have been fairly presented therein. Likewise, proper and efficient distribution of the purchased materials for the projects may be compromised to a certain extent.

4.7	We recommended the Management require the Property Officer to prepare the IIRUP and initiate proper disposal to avoid further deterioration of the properties. Proper segregation must be made of these properties that are still saleable and which can be subjected to destruction.
5.7	We recommend the Management to undertake all the procedures under COA Circular 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 to come up with the reconciled and correct balances of PPE accounts.

6.6	We recommend that Management instruct the Municipal Treasurer to submit their reports on time to the Accounting Office, and the Municipal Accountant to ensure the on-time remittance of barangay shares from RPT collections in the succeeding quarters as provided under Section 271 (d) of the Local Government Code.

7.8	We recommend and Management agreed to direct the LDRRM Officer to properly monitor the actual implementation of the PPAs included in the AIP as well as the actual utilization of the fund. Be sure to consider all the on-going and not yet implemented PPAs in the next plan preparation. Likewise, direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure the control of the expenditures to what is planned and appropriated.

8.5	We recommended and the Management agreed to direct the concerned personnel to pay the electric bill on time to avoid the incurrence of penalties in the future. Moreso, the surcharges/penalties that have been paid already, the persons responsible for its incurrence shall be made to refund the same.

9.5	We recommended that Management strictly adheres to the provisions prescribed in the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184 pertaining to warranty security, not only in infrastructure projects but also in the procurement of goods. Henceforth, ensure that future procurements be deducted with at least 1% but not more than 5% retention money or require a special bank guarantee of the same amount, otherwise any expenses due to the defects within the warranty period be charged to concerned officials.

10.13	We recommend that Management instruct the OIC - MHO Submit copies of DTRs and accomplishment reports, along with the approved working arrangements and area of assignment of the eligible HRHs; and the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all claims against government funds shall be supported with complete documentation as provided by Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445.
In view of the foregoing, we recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries to take up the Assets Account and `recognize the depreciation.
observations.managementAction: The Office of the Municipal Accountant has already recorded the recommending entries as stated in the Audit Observation Memorandum. The Audit Team Leader has already been furnished with a copy of Journal Entry Voucher
observations.reason: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management be updated with the new guidelines that are necessary for the operations, especially during the Emergency Phase. Prepare and issue Acknowledgement Receipt for In-Kind Donations to all future receipts to acknowledge the donations and proper accounting and recording. Issuance Form for In-Kind Donations or any proof of receipt must be prepared to prove the distribution to the intended recipient. And monthly reports on the receipt and distribution/utilization/issuance of in-kind donations for all in-kind donations by a private entity in compliance with COA Circular 2020-009 dated April 21, 2020 and strictly follow the provisions thereto.
observations.managementAction: The Office of the Mayor gave instructions to the offices concerned to properly account all In-kind donations, make acknowledgment thereof, prepare the reports as well as the list of beneficiaries.
observations.reason: Partially Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct LDRRM Officer to be updated with the new issuance related to DRRM policies and guidelines. Prepare and submit the one-time report of the donations received, distributed and balances to the Office of the Civil Defense, copy furnished the Auditor's office as required in COA Circular 2020-009.
observations.managementAction: The LDRRMO Office, through Mr. Artemio Cinco was instructed to prepare the One-time report and submit the same to the National Disaster Office.
observations.reason: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Management to coordinate the personnel from different departments involved in the specific operation to have the proper flow of the needed documents. Require the LRDDMO to immediately submit the complete master/distribution list of recipients of the said program to the office for further assessment and examination of the transaction's validity.
observations.managementAction: The LDRRMO is presently coordinating with the MSWDO with regards to the Other Barangay List of Recipients of various goods in response for COVID 19 pandemic
observations.reason: Partially Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Management instruct the Bids and Awards Committee to secure documentary requirements, especially during Emergency Purchase, so as not to compromise integrity and transparency in public procurement that could be disadvantageous to the government.
observations.managementAction: For compliance, we are submitting the lacking documents mentioned in the Audit Observation Memorandum.
observations.reason: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Management to direct the Bids and Awards Committee to properly prepare its Annual Procurement Plan (APP) in the correct format, and any changes therein or any supplemental reports made should be submitted to the GPPB for posting requirements to promote accountability and transparency in the conduct of Emergency Procurement.
observations.managementAction: The BAC is already aware and follows all the recommendation stated in the Audi Observation Memorandum.
observations.reason: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management strictly adheres to the National Government's guidelines in the utilization of funds received, such as in this case, to strengthen further measures in countering this pandemic and for future similar occurrences.
observations.managementAction: Rest assured that the Management shall strictly adhere to the National Guidelines, and be updated to all issuances, Guidelines and Circulars to avoid such mistake again.
observations.reason: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Management to: 

Use the required format of a sequentially numbered trip ticket duly approved by the Agency Head or the authorized representative for proper monitoring of fuel consumption with complete information being filled out in every detail needed to support the claims for payment of fuel expenses. 

Enforce the "No Driver's Trip Ticket, No Withdrawal Policy." 

Direct the authorized personnel to reconcile every month the driver's trip ticket with the Monthly Fuel consumption and Summary of Monthly Report of official Travels as mandated under COA Circular No. 77-61 D.6 to determine whether the said fuel usage is valid and is being used appropriately.

Install controls on the monitoring of issuance of the fuel and strictly adhere to the guidelines on the use of government vehicles.
observations.managementAction: It is only recently that fuel consumption control is implemented. The Office of the Mayor, which is the one In-charge, is still reviewing the rules on its implementation.
observations.reason: Partially Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management strictly impose the collection of the balances and the surcharges from the delinquent stallholders.
observations.managementAction: The Office of the Mayor sent demand letters to, and received by the Market tenants. Verification from the LEE Cashier, out of ₱80,421.25 collectibles, only ₱4,756.00 were collected``
observations.reason: Partially Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to trace the unidentified book reconciling items and effect the following adjusting entry in the books to reflect the valid and identified book reconciling items so as to present the correct balances of the account Cash-in-Bank Local Currency, Current Account.
observations.managementAction: The ₱829,785.70 was already adjusted in the Books of account. The ₱178,673.52 unidentified book reconciling items can no longer be traced since most of the Bank Reconciliation Statements during the time my Predecessor, Mrs. Lucilla G. Robillos are unavailable.
observations.reason: Partially Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer ensure accurate assessment of Business Taxes by following the proper procedures as specified in the Local Treasury Operations Manual (LTOM) and by checking of books of accounts and/or ledger of business establishment with corresponding Sworn Statement of Gross Receipts/Sales and Income Tax Return in accordance to Items (d) & € of Section2C.04 of the Municipal Revised Revenue Code of 2016. Moreover, correct the erroneous interpretation in the computation of taxes based on the Tax Table.
observations.managementAction: The Office of the Municipal Treasurer has started to coordinate with the Local Business Establishments in checking their Gross Sales and the correct figures be reported to the government.
observations.reason: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommend Management to submit justification on the excess of the Contract Cost to the COA Evaluated Cost aggregate amount of ₱1,807,580.45 for further evaluation. We also recommend Management to ensure compliance with the existing rules and regulations in the preparation of Approved Budget Cost (ABC) and that cost estimates be in accordance with the project’s Plans and Specifications.
observations.managementAction: The Justification letter was already sent to your end last April 2020.
observations.reason: Fully Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accounting Office and Municipal Treasurer’s Office to render an accounting on the result of recently conducted physical inventory and reconcile differences of the two records.

For Loans Receivable-Trust Fund, send demand letters to the debtors and impose strict collection of the amount due.  2. For Loans Receivable-General Fund, take the necessary procedures in the write-off of dormant accounts as provided in COA Circular 2016-005.

observations.recommendation: In view of the foregoing, we recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to take the following entries:

We recommended that Management be updated with the new guidelines that are necessary for the operations, especially during the Emergency Phase. Prepare and issue Acknowledgement Receipt for In-Kind Donations to all future receipts to acknowledge the donations and proper accounting and recording. Issuance Form for In-Kind Donations or any proof of receipt must be prepared to prove the distribution to the intended recipient. And monthly reports on the receipt and distribution/utilization/issuance of in-kind donations for all in-kind donations by a private entity in compliance with COA Circular 2020-009 dated April 21, 2020 and strictly follow the provisions thereto.

We recommended that Management direct LDRRM Officer to be updated with the new issuance related to DRRM policies and guidelines. Prepare and submit the one-time report of the donations received, distributed and balances to the Office of the Civil Defense, copy furnished the Auditor's office as required in COA Circular 2020-009.

We recommended the Management to coordinate the personnel from different departments involved in the specific operation to have the proper flow of the needed documents. Require the LRDDMO to immediately submit the complete master/distribution list of recipients of the said program to the office for further assessment and examination of the transaction's validity.

We recommended the Management instruct the Bids and Awards Committee to secure documentary requirements, especially during Emergency Purchase, so as not to compromise integrity and transparency in public procurement that could be disadvantageous to the government.

The Management assured the Audit Team that they will comply with the audit recommendation.

We recommended the Management to direct the Bids and Awards Committee to properly prepare its Annual Procurement Plan (APP) in the correct format, and any changes therein or any supplemental reports made should be submitted to the GPPB for posting requirements to promote accountability and transparency in the conduct of Emergency Procurement.

The Management is amenable to this audit observation and likewise assured to comply with its implementation.

We recommended that the Management strictly adheres to the National Government's guidelines in the utilization of funds received, such as in this case, to strengthen further measures in countering this pandemic and for future similar occurrences.

The Management admitted the noted deficiency and assured its adherence to the  National Government’s guidelines on fund utilization in the ensuing years.

We recommended the Management to:
Use the required format of a sequentially numbered trip ticket duly approved by the Agency Head or the authorized representative for proper monitoring of fuel consumption with complete information being filled out in every detail needed to support the claims for payment of fuel expenses.
Enforce the "No Driver's Trip Ticket, No Withdrawal Policy."
Direct the authorized personnel to reconcile every month the driver's trip ticket with the Monthly Fuel consumption and Summary of Monthly Report of official Travels as mandated under COA Circular No. 77-61 D.6 to determine whether the said fuel usage is valid and is being used appropriately.
Install controls on the monitoring of issuance of the fuel and strictly adhere to the guidelines on the use of government vehicles.

The Management admitted that they failed to examine the prescribed format of the trip ticket and the related documentary requirements needed for the payment. Nonetheless, they agreed to implement this audit recommendation and assured the audit team of its compliance for the ensuing years.

We recommended that Management strictly impose the collection of the balances and the surcharges from the delinquent stallholders.

The Management agreed to implement the audit recommendation and will send demand letters to delinquent stallholders copy furnish the Office of the Auditor.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to trace the unidentified book reconciling items and effect the following adjusting entry in the books to reflect the valid and identified book reconciling items so as to present the correct balances of the account Cash-in-Bank Local Currency, Current Account.
observations.managementAction: The P829,785.70 was already adjusted in the Books of account. The P178,673.52 unidentified book reconciling items can no longer be traced since most of the Bank Reconciliation Statements during the time my Predecessor, Mrs. Lucilla G. Robillos are unavailable.
observations.reason: Partially Implemented

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer ensure accurate assessment of business taxes by following the proper procedures as specified in the Local Treasury Operations Manual (LTOM) and by checking of books of accounts and/or ledger of business establishment with corresponding Sworn Statement of Gross Receipts/Sales and Income Tax Return in accordance to Items (d) & (e) of Section 2C.04 of the Municipal Revised Revenue Code of 2016. The Management agreed and assured that the correct assessment of the business taxes will be implemented in their 2020 operations.

We recommended Management to submit justification on the excess of the Contract Cost to the COA Evaluated Cost aggregate amount of P1,807,580.45 for further evaluation. We also recommend Management to ensure compliance with the existing rules and regulations in the preparation of Approved Budget Cost (ABC) and that cost estimates be in accordance with the project’s plans and specifications.

Management commented that they appreciated the above observation and findings truthfully for the benefit and transparency of government implemented projects. However, they accepted that this was an honest mistake on their part as they recently implemented DUPA. The submitted justifications and cost-analyses will be referred to the COA Technical Audit Specialists for further verification.

observations.recommendation:
It is recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

1.	Direct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to submit copies of government contracts, purchase orders and their supporting documents to the Commission on Audit within five (5) working days from its execution pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.

2.	Also direct the BAC to submit to the Office of the Auditor the unsubmitted contracts stated above and each of all the documents forming part thereof within five (5) days upon receipt of this Audit Observation Memorandum in order to avoid audit suspensions on the above-stated transactions.
observations.reason: Fully implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management direct the Inventory Committee to conduct physical count of Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE) which shall be reconciled with the Accounting records.  Thereafter,  submit   the   Report  of  the  Physical  Count  of  Property,  Plant  and   Equipment (RPCPPE) to the Office of the Auditor within the time frame  specified in the regulation.
observations.reason: Reiterated in CY 2017 and 2018 AAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management refrain from charging wages and honorarium for job order personnel performing regular and administrative functions. The municipal officials are enjoined to strictly adhere to the guidelines set under Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011- 1 of the DBM and DILG in the appropriation and utilization of the 20% Development Fund to ensure its optimal utilization.

We recommended that the Management appropriate one percent of the Local Government Unit’s Internal Revenue Allotment as required in Section 15 of Republic Act 9344 and to prepare the Local Development Plan and Local Investment Plan for Children with programs, projects and activities that promote and protect the rights of children.
observations.reason: By attribution

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management take appropriate action for the collection of the loans receivable and instruct the Municipal Accountant to initiate the necessary procedures for the writing off of those balances or accounts to present fairly the Receivable Account in the Financial Statement.
observations.reason: At present, the MTO is occupied with the updating of the taxpayer’s ledger to increase tax collection.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management direct the Municipal Accounting Office and Municipal Treasurer’s Office to render an accounting of the result of recently conducted physical inventory and reconcile differences of the two records.

We recommended that the Management take the following actions:
For Loans Receivable-Trust Fund, send demand letters to the debtors and impose strict collection of the amount due.
For Loans Receivable-General Fund, take the necessary procedures in the write-off of dormant accounts as provided in COA Circular 2016-005.
We recommended that the Management impose the collection of the liquidated damages from the supplier amounting to P1,682,613.63

We recommended that the BAC Secretariat facilitate the posting of the NOA and NTP in the PhilGEPS and in any conspicuous place in the premises of the agency as provided in Sections 37.1.6 and 37.4.2 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184.

We recommended that the GFPS use the GAD Plan and Budget in the formulation of the GAD Accomplishment Report as required in the above-cited regulation to facilitate the evaluation and monitoring on the implementation of GAD PPAs.

We recommended that Management:
1. 	Direct the Contractor, thru the Municipal Engineer, to fast track the implementation of the project and impose liquidated damages for the delay, if warranted.
2. 	Require the Municipal Engineer to regularly conduct an inspection of the projects implemented and prepare SWA every end of the month to monitor its status/progress.
3. 	Inform the DILG-CARAGA of the project status as stipulated in the MOA.
We recommended that the Management strictly observe the implementation of Ordinance No 03 series of 2003 to facilitate efficiency in the operations of the Lanuza Water System.

We recommended that the Management direct the Local Finance Committee to meticulously and judiciously plan in order to achieve the proper allocation of expenditures for each development activity. Moreover, the Local Development Council identify priority projects that partake the nature of investment or capital expenditures.

observations.reason: PPMPs were submitted to the LGU BAC for consolidation/ incorporation with the Annual Procurement Plan/ Supplemental Procurement Plan

observations.recommendation: We recommended   that   management adhere strictly to the provisions of the COA Circular No. 2012-002 by submitting the Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF to COA within the legal time frame.
observations.reason: Reports were submitted

observations.recommendation: We recommended management to ensure the compliance on the standards   and   requirements for collection and transport of solid   waste pursuant to Section 24 of RA No. 9003.
observations.managementAction:
1. Separate Collection schedule for Residual waste / non-biodegradable wastes for composting.

2. Installation of net as cover for collected waste during collection and transport.

3. Twinning the garbage receptacle/sack to avoid spillage of waste during transport.
observations.reason: Complied

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management strictly comply with the minimum standards and operating requirements of a controlled dump in accordance to Sections 39 and 41 of RA 9003.
observations.managementAction:
1. To cover the garbage pit / dumpsite with earth.
2. Fencing of the dumpsite to ensure safety from intrusion of stray animals.

3. Gravelling of the dumpsite access road.

4. Apply for Special Patent for proposed SLF site.
observations.reason:
Covering of the dumpsite is required every collection. However, due to the cost of operation in covering the dump site, the LGU practice of covering the dump when it is full.

The LGU is awaiting for the approval for their application for the special patent for the propose area for sanitary landfill.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management ensure the compliance on the guidelines for establishment of Materials Recovery Facility (MRF) pursuant to Section 33 of RA 9003.
observations.managementAction:
1. Repair of the MRF.

2. Disposal of Recycled materials.

3. Crashing empty unsold glass bottles using shredding machine.
observations.reason: Complied

observations.recommendation:
It is recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

1.	Direct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to submit copies of government contracts, purchase orders and their supporting documents to the Commission on Audit within five (5) working days from its execution pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.

2.	Also direct the BAC to submit to the Office of the Auditor the unsubmitted contracts stated above and each of all the documents forming part thereof within five (5) days upon receipt of this Audit Observation Memorandum in order to avoid audit suspensions on the above-stated transactions.
observations.reason: In CY 2017 and 2018, it is still noted that contracts/ POs were still submitted late.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Offices in-charge in the preparation of purchase orders to completely indicate all the necessary information relative to the purchase.  In addition, direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly impose the policy that procured items with incompletely filled up POs/contracts shall not be processed for payment.
observations.reason: Complete data on POs

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Treasurer to complete the conduct of Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE) to be reconciled with the Accounting records.  Thereafter, submit the Report of the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) to the Office of the Auditor.
observations.reason: The MTO already conducted physical count but subject for reconciliation with the record

observations.recommendation: We recommended Management to direct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the intra-agency receivables and intra-payables accounts and effect adjustments to reflect the correct account balances thus ensuring the presentation of reliable date on the financial reports of the LGU as of a given period of time.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant has partially reconciled the said account

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management remit immediately the unutilized balances of the Priority Development Assistance Fund (PDAF) released in CY 2013 and prior years to the Bureau of the Treasury.
observations.reason: Complied

observations.recommendation: We recommended Management to follow up and impose the collection of the liquidated damages from the supplier.
observations.reason: Management is still communicating the matter to the supplier

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the GAD Focal Person to include in the submission of the GAD AR the reports /documents mentioned in Section 4 C.8 (5) of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 to facilitate the monitoring, evaluation and substantiation of the reported accomplishments.
observations.reason: The GAD Focal Person is doing the best she can to comply the necessary supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the GFPS to administer HGDG for those LGU programs and projects attributed wholly or a portion thereof during the preparation of the GAD plan and budget and the accomplishment report.

We recommended that Management create the mandatory positions for LDRRMO and appoint personnel who met the qualification standards set in the NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC Joint Circular (JMC) 2014-1 dated April 4, 2014 in order to institutionalize the LDRRMO’s organizational structure and manpower compliment aimed to carry out the DRRM plans and programs in the long run.
We recommended strict adherence to the provisions of Section 37 of Republic Act No. 9003 on the use of Sanitary Landfill.
observations.reason: The necessary documents are still being worked out.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council (LDRRMC) to reprogram the unexpended LDRRM Fund that will strengthen and improve the capacity of the LGU to reduce and mitigate disaster risks and to immediately respond to the needs of its constituents stricken by calamities and to incorporate the said fund in the annual LDRRMFIP of the LGU.

We recommended that management direct the Inventory Committee to conduct physical count of Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE) which shall be reconciled with the Accounting records.  Thereafter,  submit   the   Report  of  the  Physical  Count  of  Property,  Plant  and   Equipment (RPCPPE) to the Office of the Auditor within the time frame  specified in the regulation.
observations.reason: Reiterated in CY 2017 AAR.

The municipal officials are enjoined to strictly adhere to the guidelines set under Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011- 1 of the DBM and DILG in the appropriation and utilization of the 20% Development Fund to ensure its optimal utilization.

Thereafter, submit the Report of the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) to the Office of the Auditor.

We recommended Management to direct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the intra-agency receivables and intra-payables accounts and effect adjustments to reflect the correct account balances thus ensuring the presentation of reliable date on the financial reports of the LGU as of a given period of time.
We recommended that Management remit immediately the unutilized balances of the Priority Development Assistance Fund (PDAF) released in CY 2013 and prior years to the Bureau of the Treasury.

We recommended that Management require the GAD Focal Person to include in the submission of the GAD AR the reports /documents mentioned in Section 4 C.8 (5) of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 to facilitate the monitoring, evaluation and substantiation of the reported accomplishments.

We recommended that the Management take appropriate action for the collection of the loans receivable and instruct the Municipal Accountant to initiate the necessary procedures for the writing off of those balances or accounts to present fairly the Receivable Account in the Financial Statement.
We recommended strict adherence to the provisions of Section 37 of Republic Act No. 9003 on the use of Sanitary Landfill.

We recommended that the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council (LDRRMC) to reprogram the unexpended LDRRM Fund that will strengthen and improve the capacity of the LGU to reduce and mitigate disaster risks and to immediately respond to the needs of its constituents stricken by calamities and to incorporate the said fund in the annual LDRRMFIP of the LGU.

observations.recommendation:
It is recommended that:

1.	The Municipal Engineer prepare the Program of Work of each project implemented and see to it that duly validated accomplishment reports are attached to the payrolls before these are processed for payment;

2.	The disbursing officer/treasurer submit to the accounting office liquidations with complete documentation; and

3.	The Municipal Accountant return the liquidation reports submitted with incomplete documentation to the Accountable Officer for compliance.

observations.recommendation:
It is recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

1.	Direct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to submit copies of government contracts, purchase orders and their supporting documents to the Commission on Audit within five (5) working days from its execution pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.

2. 	Also direct the BAC to submit to the Office of the Auditor the unsubmitted contracts stated above and each of all the documents forming part thereof within five (5) days upon receipt of this Audit Observation Memorandum in order to avoid audit suspensions on the above-stated transactions.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended that fund transfers from the 20% Development Fund be supported with documents such as project proposals or program of expenditures to establish the validity of the transfer.   Any transfer of fund for expenditures not chargeable to the 20% Development Fund as provided under Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011, issued by the Department of Interior and Local Government (DILG) and the Department of Budget and Management (DBM) should not be allowed.

Moreover, require the Municipal Accountant to deduct from the final payment to the contractor the excess amount of P486,637.07.

observations.recommendation: We recommend to require the BAC to prepare regularly the Procurement Monitoring Report (see attached PMR Form) for all procurement activities of the municipality for submission to the GPBB every semester as required under Section 12.2 of the IRR of RA 9184.  A printed copy of the report shall be furnished to this Office to allow the audit team to verify the status of procurement activities specified in the Annual Procurement Plan as basis for auditor’s action, if any.

In addition, direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly impose the policy that procured items with incompletely filled up POs/contracts shall not be processed for payment.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant to coordinate and exert effort to submit immediately the accounts for the months of September to December of 2014 and succeeding reports should be submitted within the timeframe set under the said regulations.
observations.reason: Recommendations were well-taken by the Management. Regular submission of reports were made.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation to direct the following personnel/offices:

Inventory Committee:

1. 	Conduct the physical count of the Property, Plant and Equipment.
2. 	Prepare the Report on the Physical Count of PPE by type showing the balance of property and equipment per cards and per count and the shortage/overage, if any.
3. 	Prepare the report yearly in three copies, certified correct by the Inventory Committee and approved by the Head of the Agency, and submit to the Office of the Auditor concerned not later than January 31 of each year.  

Accounting Office:

1. 	Reconcile the report of the inventory committee with the control accounts in the general ledger.
2. 	Determine the discrepancy and effect adjustments in the books, if warranted.
3. 	Maintain and update regularly the Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards (PPELC).

Municipal Treasurer/Property Officer:

1. 	Prepare the inventory guidelines, indicating the date of physical inventory, who will supervise the count, the personnel to act as counters and checkers, and the sequence of the counting, for approval by the Municipal Mayor.
2. 	Maintain and update regularly the Property/Equipment Cards for each class of fixed asset.
observations.reason: No physical count of PPE and non-maintenance of PPE ledger cards and property cards were still noted, thus reiterated in CY 2016 AAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended management to direct the Municipal Accountant to submit the liquidation reports of the cash advances recorded as liquidated per attached list for auditor’s verification.
observations.managementAction: No time in digging up files in the stock room but we will settle this the soonest possible.
observations.reason: As of December 31, 2016, the said liquidation reports were still not submitted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend directing the following personnel/ Offices:

Inventory Committee:

Conduct the physical count of the Property, Plant and Equipment.
Prepare the Report on the Physical Count of PPE by type showing the balance of property and equipment per cards and per count and the shortage/overage, if any.
Prepare the report yearly in three copies, certified correct by the Inventory Committee and approved by the Head of the Agency, and submit to the Office of the Auditor concerned not later than January 31 of each year.  For CY 2014, submit the report on or before March 31, 2015.

Accounting Office:

Reconcile the report of the inventory committee with the control accounts in the general ledger.
Determine the discrepancy and effect adjustments in the books, if warranted.
Maintain and update regularly the Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards (PPELC)

Municipal Treasurer/ Property Officer:

Prepare the inventory guidelines, indicating the date of physical inventory, who is to supervise the count, the personnel to act as counters and checkers, and the sequence of the counting, for approval by the Municipal Mayor.
Maintain and update regularly the Property/Equipment Cards for each class of fixed asset.
observations.reason:
Inventory taking was not conducted as of December 2016.

Reiterated in this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommend to require the BAC to prepare regularly the Procurement Monitoring Report (see attached PMR Form) for all procurement activities of the municipality for submission to the GPBB every semester as required under Section 12.2 of the IRR of RA 9184.  A printed copy of the report shall be furnished to this Office to allow the audit team to verify the status of procurement activities specified in the Annual Procurement Plan as basis for auditor’s action, if any.

Direct the Municipal Accountant to submit the necessary supporting documents for further verification.
Strictly adhere to the provisions  COA Circular No. 2012-001 and Budget Circular No. 2004-5A.
We recommended that management stop immediately the practice  of  transferring  funds  for   personal,  religious  and  private purposes  	pursuant Section 335 of RA No. 7160.

We recommended that management:

1.	Direct the Municipal Accountant to adjust the stale checks by debiting the amount to Cash in Bank-LCCA and crediting Accounts Payable.

2. 	In the preparation of BRS, indicate complete details of the reconciling items for verification purposes, and attach documents to support validity of the reconciling items such as debit and credit memos or other documents.

3. 	For bank reconciling items, furnish the bank copies of the BRS and inform them in writing of the errors that need adjustment in their books.
observations.reason: As of December 31, 2016, the Municipal Accountant implemented the recommendations made by the audit team. BRS were regularly submitted except for the November 2016 wherein the bank was unable to provide bank statements to its depositors.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to give comments for each violation committed on the above-stated procurement and to revisit, familiarize and comply the provisions of the Revised IRR of RA 9184 in the conduct of public bidding for future transactions. Bidding procedures and documents should be reviewed thoroughly to conform with the procurement guidelines to avoid conflicting statements.
observations.reason: As of December 31, 2016, the management implemented the recommendations made by the audit team.

It is recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

1. 	Direct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to submit copies of government contracts, purchase orders and their supporting documents to the Commission on Audit within five (5) working days from its execution pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.

2. 	Also direct the BAC to submit to the Office of the Auditor the unsubmitted contracts stated above and each of all the documents forming part thereof within five (5) days upon receipt of this Audit Observation Memorandum in order to avoid audit suspensions on the above-stated transactions.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended that the Municipal Treasurer monitor strictly the issuance of accountable forms and instruct all accountable officers to render Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms (RAAF) and subsequently prepare the Consolidated Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms (CRAAF) every end of the month.  Attach also in the CRAAF breakdown of all issuances made to effectively monitor the receipt, issuance, and cancellation of all accountable forms in the position of each accountable officers .
observations.reason: The Municipal Treasurer implemented the recommendations made by the audit team. The report were already attached monthly report on collections and deposits.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended that the Municipal Treasurer direct all collectors to stop the practice of transferring accountable forms as prescribed under COA Circular No. 92-382 to easily monitor the issuance of accountable forms.
observations.reason: The collectors already stopped the practice of transfer of accountable forms from one accountable officer to another.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended that fund transfers from the 20% Development Fund be supported with documents such as project proposals or program of expenditures to establish the validity of the transfer.   Any transfer of fund for expenditures not chargeable to the 20% Development Fund as provided under Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011, issued by the Department of Interior and Local Government (DILG) and the Department of Budget and Management (DBM) should not be allowed.
observations.reason: Fund transfers were still noted to be made without the complete documentation, thus reiterated in CY 2016 AAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that in the preparation of the SEF budget, the members of the Local School Board give priority to programs, activities and projects enumerated in Section 272 of RA 7160 and DECS, DBM and DILG Joint Circulars Nos. 01, 01-A and 01-B and refrain from charging expenditures which are not prescribed by law for which SEF can be disposed of.
observations.reason: There were still expenditures that were charged to the fund which are not prescribed by law for which SEF can be disposed of.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the person in charge of the preparation of Approved Budget for the Contract (ABC) to strictly follow the revised guidelines of DPWH Department Order No. 22, series of 2015, in order to judiciously determine the ABC beneficial to the government.  Moreover, require the Municipal Accountant to deduct from the final payment to the contractor the excess amount of P486,637.07.
observations.reason: Deduction for the excess cost was not yet applied as there was no final payment made as of December 31, 2016.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management:

1. 	Require all implementing offices to submit Quarterly Reports on Government Projects, Programs and Activities funded from all sources to the Office of the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator who will consolidate the reports for submission to municipal officials for monitoring and decision making purposes.

2. 	Conduct regular periodic meetings with the heads of departments/offices to assess whether the implementation of PPAs is proceeding as planned and to ensure that implementation constraints, if any, are immediately resolved.
observations.reason: The management implemented the recommendations made by the audit team. Projects implemented by the Municipality were included in the quarterly reports for government projects/programs and activities.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management submit Request for Contract Review on the procurement of Brand New Heavy Equipment together with its supporting documents.  For future procurement activity, comply strictly the regulations prescribed under IRR of RA 9184 to avoid possible suspension or disallowance in audit.
observations.reason: The management implemented the recommendations made by the audit team. Contracts of succeeding projects were submitted to the Office of the Audit Team Leader for contract review.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Offices in-charge in the preparation of purchase orders to completely indicate all the necessary information relative to the purchase.  In addition, direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly impose the policy that procured items with incompletely filled up POs/contracts shall not be processed for payment.
observations.reason: Several POs were noted with incomplete information.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management stop the practice of appropriating funds for unrelated expenses under the 20% Development Fund. The municipal officials should strictly adhere to the guidelines set under DBM and DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 in the appropriation and utilization of the 20% Development Fund to ensure its optimal utilization.
observations.reason: Administrative expenses charged to 20% DF were still noted.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant to coordinate and exert effort to submit immediately the accounts for the months of September to December of 2014 and succeeding reports should be submitted within the timeframe set under the said regulations.
observations.reason: The November 2016 accounts were not submitted as of December 31, 2016.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management prepare their APP in accordance with the guidelines set forth in Section 7, Rule II of the Revised IRR 9184 taking into account the different programs, objects and activities to ensure that supplies, materials and equipment needed in the operation of the agency are included in the programmed procurement for the year. In the event that the needed supplies and materials are not programmed/included in the APP, a supplementary or amendatory procurement plan should be prepared.
observations.reason: As of December 31, 2016, the Annual Procurement Plan for CY 2016 showed that procured drugs and medicines were included in the plan.

observations.recommendation: We recommend to advice the BAC Secretariat, through the BAC Chairman strict compliance to the prescribed period of submission of purchase order  and its supporting documents to  the  Office of  the  Auditor  pursuant to  COA Circular 2009 – 001dated February 12, 2009, to give the audit team enough time for its review and evaluation.  Further, advice the Supply Officer to furnish the office with the delivery receipt and inspection report within 24 hours from date of delivery pursuant to existing regulations.
observations.reason: Not all purchase orders were submitted on time.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that:

a. 	The Municipal Accountant submits promptly all paid DVs and payrolls and their supporting documents pursuant to Section 39 (1), (3) of PD 1445, and COA Circular No. 95-006.

b. 	The Municipal Treasurer forwards the DVs to the Accounting Office as soon as the corresponding checks are released to the suppliers, to ensure timely submission thereof to the Auditor.
observations.managementAction: Accounts for CY 2016 were already submitted promptly except for November and December 2016 which remain unsubmitted as of December 31, 2016.
observations.reason: Accounts for the month of November 2016 were not submitted as of December 31, 2016 due to the inability of the bank to produce Bank Statements to its depositors.

observations.recommendation: We recommend the Local School Board to strictly observe the provisions of Joint Circular Nos. 01, 01-A and 01-B  in prioritizing expenses to be charged to the fund to ensure that goals and objectives in the imposition of the additional one percent (1%) tax on real property accruing to the SEF are achieved.
observations.reason: Reiterated in CY 2015 and 2016.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management refrain from or exercise prudence in incurring food expenses during meetings, sessions or hearings to be responsive to the needs of the constituents and to ensure that government resources are used equitably and wisely.
observations.reason: Recommendations made by the Audit team were implemented by the management.
